notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 1, Book 2 source file.
Early card: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start.

book.title: The Little Engine That Could
book.description: The Little Engine That Could includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start

weeks.title: The Little Engine That Could Book Card
vocabulary: 1. carrying - taking something from one place to another
1. important - having great value or meaning
2. freight - goods or cargo carried by train
1. volunteers - offers to help without being asked
2. steep - having a sharp slope; very hard to climb
1. determined - having made up your mind to do something
2. struggled - tried very hard to do something difficult
1. strength - the power to do hard things
2. echoed - bounced back as a repeated sound
1. success - doing what you set out to do
2. proud - feeling good about what you have done
journal: Write about a challenge that felt hard at first. What helped you keep trying?
Explain the lesson of the story using the words effort, courage, and helpfulness.
writing: Write about a challenge that felt hard at first. What helped you keep trying?
Explain the lesson of the story using the words effort, courage, and helpfulness.
questions: 1. Why wouldn't the passenger engine help?
2. What excuse did the freight engine give?
1. What color was the little engine?
2. Had the Little Blue Engine ever pulled a train over a mountain before?
1. What made the train hard to pull?
2. How did the toys help the Little Blue Engine?
1. What did the Little Blue Engine keep saying to herself?
2. Who started saying the words with the Little Blue Engine?
1. What did the Little Blue Engine say as she went down the mountain?
2. What lesson did the Little Blue Engine learn?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
